This commit enables local context (Symf) for all users, including Enterprise users.

The previous restriction that limited local context to DotCom and Enterprise Starter users has been removed. This change ensures that all authenticated users can benefit from local context features.

Something to be aware of, not having the project into enterprise instance will display current codebase (not available)
![Loom Screenshot 2025-06-06 at 05 49 57](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a8824bd4-1b94-4040-a930-3f72afb18be6)



## Test plan
- Check that symf is enabled for enterprise user
lint fixes

fix: Improve image migration script and handle missing UIDs

This commit improves the image migration script introduced in the previous commit by:

- Adding a check for missing UIDs when converting image paths to UIDs. A warning is logged if a UID cannot be found, and the migration continues to the next image.
- Improving logging messages to be more specific about the block type being updated (e.g., "hero image" instead of just "item").
- Adding try-except blocks around `transaction.commit()` calls to handle potential exceptions during the commit process. If a commit fails, the transaction is aborted, and the exception is re-raised.
- Adding a final log message to indicate the completion status of the migration and the total number of objects processed.

Merge branch '287497' into image_scales

fix(volto-object-widget): enable coverage and fix cypress ci

This commit enables coverage collection for Cypress tests and fixes the Cypress CI configuration.

The following changes were made:

- Added `CYPRESS_COVERAGE=true` to the `cypress-open` and `cypress-run` commands in the Makefile to enable coverage collection during local development and CI runs.
- Added `--env coverage=true` to the `cypress-run` command to ensure coverage is enabled when running tests.
- Added `RAZZLE_CI=true` to the `cypress-ci` command to ensure the correct environment variables are set during CI runs. This fixes an issue where the tests were failing in CI due to missing environment variables.

feat(e2e): Add feature flag control to mock server and tests

This commit introduces the ability to control feature flags within the mock server and e2e tests.

- Added `setFeatureFlag` method to the `MockServer` class to allow setting feature flag values.
- Modified the GraphQL endpoint handling for `EvaluateFeatureFlags` to return the configured feature flag values.
- Added a new endpoint `/.test/featureFlags/refresh` to signal feature flag refresh.
- Updated the "editing a chat message with @-mention" and "Add Selection to Cody Chat" e2e tests to use the `no-default-repo-chip` feature flag to prevent workspace context. This ensures tests are isolated and don't rely on workspace context.

fix(e2e): Improve repository context in chat and update mock server

This commit improves the handling of repository context in chat interactions within the e2e tests. It also updates the mock server to use a more realistic code host example.

- Updates the `testWithGitRemote` helper to use `codehost.example` instead of `host.example` for the git remote origin.
- Modifies the default value of the `no-default-repo-chip` feature flag to `false` in the mock server to allow remote repository context by default.
- Adds a test case to `mention-repository.test.ts` to verify that remote repositories are available via the mention menu.
- Updates the `initial-context.test.ts` to correctly assert the initial context with the updated mock server and feature flag configuration.
- Sets the `no-default-repo-chip` feature flag to `true` in `chat-atFile.test.ts` to prevent workspace context and ensure test isolation.
